input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

<current_datetime>2026-04-24T00:54:06.059Z</current_datetime>

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the approach:

## 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Resources/Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
                    x:Class="MyApp.Resources.Themes.LightTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="AccentColor">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Create matching `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` files with different color values but **the same keys**.

## 2. Load a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <Resources:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

## 3. Create a theme-switching service

```csharp
public static class ThemeService
{
    public enum AppTheme { Light, Dark, HighContrast }

    public static void ApplyTheme(AppTheme theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            AppTheme.Dark         => new Resources.Themes.DarkTheme(),
            AppTheme.HighContrast => new Resources.Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _                     => new Resources.Themes.LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("app_theme", theme.ToString());
    }

    public static void LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        if (Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(Preferences.Get("app_theme", "Light"), out var saved))
            ApplyTheme(saved);
    }
}
```

Call `ThemeService.LoadSavedTheme()` in your `App` constructor (after `InitializeComponent`).

## 4. Use `DynamicResource` in your XAML (critical)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" Text="Hello" />
    <Button BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource AccentColor}" Text="Go" />
</ContentPage>
```

`DynamicResource` (not `StaticResource`) is what makes the UI update immediately when the dictionary is swapped.

## 5. Wire up the settings page

```xml
<Picker Title="Theme" SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>High Contrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    var theme = ((Picker)sender).SelectedIndex switch
    {
        1 => ThemeService.AppTheme.Dark,
        2 => ThemeService.AppTheme.HighContrast,
        _ => ThemeService.AppTheme.Light,
    };
    ThemeService.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

**Key takeaways:**
- Every theme dictionary must share the **same keys**.
- Always bind with **`DynamicResource`** so swaps propagate instantly.
- `Preferences` persists the choice across app restarts.
- Clearing and re-adding to `MergedDictionaries` is the standard MAUI pattern for runtime theme switching.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes that can be switched at runtime from a settings page. This is a general "how-to" question, not a codebase task. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
